hero.slide1_h1:
Get your non-repayable
grant funding
across Europe
hero.slide1_sub: Up to 80% of your project funded without repayment. Access programmes reserved for European project promoters.
hero.slide2_h1:
Public and European
funds to finance
your ambitions
hero.slide2_sub: Thanks to European and national structural programmes, the State directly invests in the development of your territories.
hero.slide3_h1:
A simplified process,
personal guidance,
fast response
hero.slide3_sub: Submit your application entirely online. Our accredited experts guide you from submission to disbursement of your grant.

institutional.legal: FEDE operates within the legal framework governing European and national public funding, including ERDF/ESF+ 2021–2027. Our accredited structure ensures full regulatory compliance of every application submitted through the platform.
institutional.desc: FEDE is the official platform for applying for non-repayable funding for project promoters across Europe.
institutional.countries_desc: Access ERDF, ESF+, Cohesion Fund and dedicated national programmes across all 27 EU Member States. Each territory has specific funding envelopes tailored to its economic, cultural and sectoral realities.
institutional.advisor_desc: Every project holder is supported by a dedicated FEDE advisor, available to validate your documents, answer your questions and ensure your application is complete through to the final funding decision.

why.title: Why were these non-repayable grants created?
why.p1: The European Union has established structural funds (ERDF, ESF+, EAFRD) to support the economic and social development of its regions.
why.p2: The 2021–2027 European structural programmes mobilise hundreds of billions of euros for regional development, employment, innovation and ecological transition.
why.p3: Each Member State also has its own complementary national programmes.
why.p4: FEDE is the gateway that gives you simple, secure and guided access to this public funding.
why.card_legal_desc: Programmes under the ERDF/ESF+ 2021–2027 regulations and national legislation.
why.card_urgency_desc: The current programming period ends in 2027. Late applications risk missing this funding window.
why.card_who_desc: Entrepreneurs, farmers, associations, local authorities, craftspeople, promoters of innovative projects.
why.card_impact_desc: Thousands of projects funded annually across Europe thanks to European structural funds.

urgency.title: Why submit your application now?
urgency.sub: Every year of waiting is a missed opportunity. Here is why it is essential to act today.
urgency.r1_desc: Available funds are not unlimited. ERDF/ESF+ 2025–2027 programmes have defined ceilings.
urgency.r2_desc: Between application submission and actual disbursement, allow a minimum of 2-3 months.
urgency.r3_desc: The current European programming period ends in 2027. Late applications may no longer find eligible programmes.
urgency.cta_sub: Free registration • Online application • No travel required
urgency.cta_desc: Join the 3,500+ project promoters who have benefited from non-repayable grants through FEDE. Registration is free, the process 100% online.

projects.title: What projects can be funded?
projects.sub: Non-repayable grants cover a wide spectrum of economic, social and environmental activities.

types.business_desc: Financing of start-up costs, equipment purchases, working capital and initial productive investments.
types.innovation_desc: Support for R&D projects, process digitalisation, development of innovative technology solutions and startups.
types.agri_desc: Farms, aquaculture, artisan fishing, renewable energy, sustainable development projects.
types.housing_desc: New construction, rehabilitation of degraded housing, energy improvement and access to social housing.
types.training_desc: Insertion programmes, apprenticeships, continuing training, creation of lasting jobs.
types.culture_desc: Socially useful association projects, cultural events, sports infrastructure and civic initiatives.

process.title: How to obtain your funding?
process.sub: A structured, transparent and guided process, from submission to disbursement.

steps.s1_desc: Create your secure account in 5 minutes. Enter your project, territory, sector and requested amount.
steps.s2_desc: Upload your documents to your secure personal space: ID, proof of address, financial plan.
steps.s3_desc: Our experts analyse the compliance and eligibility of your application. When funding is confirmed, you receive an official notification.
steps.s3_badge: ✓ Agreement obtained → Confirmation fees: 456€ incl. VAT
steps.s4_desc: After fee payment and final validation, your application is forwarded to the funding body. The grant is paid directly into your bank account.

reviews.title: What our beneficiaries say
reviews.r1_text: Thanks to FEDE, I obtained €28,000 to open my shop. The process was clear and follow-up impeccable. I highly recommend it to all project holders.
reviews.r2_text: The platform is very professional and well organised. My advisor answered all my questions. I received my agricultural ERDF grant in less than 60 days. Impeccable.
reviews.r3_text: I never thought I could fund my eco-tourism project without an initial contribution. FEDE gave me access to €35,000 in ERDF funding. A scheme that truly makes a difference.
reviews.r4_text: Very satisfied with the speed of processing. The file was processed in 45 days. The only downside: the document list is long, but unavoidable for public funds.
reviews.r5_text: Our association benefited from ERDF funding for our cultural activities. The tracking space is perfect, real-time notifications are very practical.
reviews.r6_text: I renovated several social housing units thanks to ERDF funding. FEDE simplified all administrative procedures. A real time saver.
reviews.r7_text: As an independent trainer, I was able to fund my certification through ESF+. The advisor in charge of my file was attentive and competent. I recommend it 100%.
reviews.r8_text: Solar panel installation project funded at 65% by ERDF. FEDE made this project possible. Without this support, we could never have invested.

faq.title: Everything you need to know
faq.sub: Clear and precise answers to your most common questions.
faq.a1: A non-repayable grant is a financial aid given by the State or the European Union that does not have to be repaid, unlike a bank loan.
faq.q2: Who can submit an application on FEDE?
faq.a2: Any natural or legal person whose project is located in one of the countries covered by our platform.
faq.q3: What are the 456€ incl. VAT processing fees for?
faq.a3: The processing fees are regulatory administrative charges covering the cost of analysis of your application by our accredited experts. They are only issued once your funding is confirmed.
faq.a4: Once your complete application is submitted, the processing time averages 30 to 90 days.
faq.a5: Yes. We strictly comply with GDPR (EU Regulation 2016/679). Your data is encrypted and never transferred to commercial third parties.

antiscam.title: Official warning — Protect yourself from scams
antiscam.desc: Malicious individuals impersonate FEDE. What you must know:
antiscam.no_telegram: No official Telegram or Facebook channel
antiscam.no_telegram_desc: Any group or account on these networks claiming to be FEDE is a scam.
antiscam.no_wire: We never request transfers to third parties
antiscam.no_wire_desc: Be wary of any request for 'unblocking fees' or transfers to external accounts.
antiscam.no_banking: No agent contacts you for your banking details
antiscam.no_banking_desc: Our advisors will never ask you for your password or card number.
antiscam.official_desc: Check the URL in your browser. Our only communications come from @fede-financement.com.

mobile.sub: Upload documents, check the progress of your application and communicate with your advisor from your Android smartphone.

avis_reviews.name: Patrick O'Brien
avis_reviews.texte: Thanks to FEDE I secured €53,000 from the ERDF Ireland fund to fully renovate my restaurant in Dublin city centre. The process was clear, deadlines were met and follow-up was impeccable. I had a positive response within 45 days. Highly recommended.

avis_reviews.name: Maria Camilleri
avis_reviews.texte: I renovated my boutique hotel in the heart of Valletta thanks to €41,000 from the ERDF Malta programme obtained through FEDE. The platform simplified the entire bureaucratic process enormously. The assigned advisor was very professional.

avis_reviews.name: Sean Murphy
avis_reviews.texte: I converted my farm to organic production thanks to €76,000 from the EAFRD programme obtained with FEDE. The application process was well guided and the platform very intuitive. The grant was approved in 52 days. Highly recommended.

avis_reviews.name: Anna Sullivan
avis_reviews.texte: My fintech startup received €89,000 from the ERDF Atlantic Corridor fund for software development and team recruitment. FEDE simplified the European bureaucracy enormously. Thorough document preparation needed but the team guided me at every step.

avis_reviews.name: Joseph Farrugia
avis_reviews.texte: Our cultural association in Malta received €33,000 from the ERDF Cultural Heritage fund for our artistic activities and heritage programmes. The real-time tracking area is perfect, notifications very practical and the team responds quickly.

avis_reviews.name: Orla Brennan
avis_reviews.texte: Our agricultural cooperative installed a photovoltaic system thanks to €94,000 ERDF funding obtained through FEDE. Without this support we would never have been able to invest. Expected return on investment in 3 years. Excellent service.

avis_reviews.name: Michael Walsh
avis_reviews.texte: My craft pottery studio in Wexford received €24,000 through the LEADER Ireland Rural programme managed with FEDE. The process is thorough but well explained. The advisor was very accessible. Recommended to all craftspeople.

avis_reviews.name: Christina Borg
avis_reviews.texte: Our non-profit organisation received €31,000 from the ESF+ Malta fund for the creation of an advisory centre for disadvantaged youth. FEDE simplified all administrative hurdles. Highly recommended for all social organisations.

faq_full.general_1_q: What is FEDE and what is its role?
faq_full.general_1_r: FEDE is the official platform for applying and tracking non-repayable grants for project promoters residing or operating in the 27 EU member states, as well as the 5 French overseas territories: Martinique, Guadeloupe, La Réunion, New Caledonia and French Polynesia. Operating within the legal framework defined by Article L1611-2 of the General Code of Local Authorities (CGCT), our platform connects eligible project promoters with the relevant public and European funding bodies.
faq_full.general_2_r: A non-repayable grant is a financial aid awarded by the State, a local authority or the European Union, which does not have to be repaid once disbursed. Unlike a bank loan, it generates no debt, no interest charges and does not increase your financial liabilities. It is definitively acquired once the award conditions are met and the project is carried out in accordance with the plan approved by the funding body.
faq_full.general_3_q: Why do these grants exist specifically for French overseas territories?
faq_full.general_3_r: French overseas territories face structural disadvantages recognised by European law (Article 349 TFEU): geographic remoteness, insularity, dependence on imports, restricted local markets and difficulties accessing conventional bank credit. To correct these imbalances, the European Union has granted these regions preferential access to European structural funds (ERDF, ESF+, EAFRD), supplemented by specific national programmes from the French State.
faq_full.general_4_q: Which territories are covered by FEDE?
faq_full.general_4_r: FEDE covers the 27 EU member states, as well as the 5 French overseas territories: Martinique, Guadeloupe, La Réunion, New Caledonia and French Polynesia. Each territory benefits from specific programmes adapted to its economic, cultural and geographical realities.
faq_full.general_5_q: Can I apply if I don't yet live in the territory concerned?
faq_full.general_5_r: In general, the project promoter must be domiciled in the territory concerned or have their registered office there. However, some programmes allow exceptions for installation projects. We recommend creating your account and submitting your application so that our experts can analyse your specific situation.
faq_full.eligibilite_1_r: Applications can be submitted by: adult natural persons (sole traders, micro-entrepreneurs, liberal professions), legal entities (SARL, SAS, SA, EURL, cooperatives), associations or their local equivalents, local authorities and their public bodies, economic interest groups, farmers and agricultural groups, and craftspeople. The main condition is that the promoter is established or resides in one of the covered countries or territories.
faq_full.eligibilite_2_q: What types of projects are eligible?
faq_full.eligibilite_2_r: Eligible projects cover a wide spectrum: business creation and development, innovation and digital transformation, agriculture and fishing, environment and renewable energy, sustainable tourism, housing and social housing, professional training and integration, culture and sport, health and social action, and local public interest association projects.
faq_full.eligibilite_3_q: Can my project be refused? For what reasons?
faq_full.eligibilite_3_r: Yes, an application can be refused or declared inadmissible in several cases: the project does not fall within the eligible sector, the promoter does not meet residency or legal status requirements, the project is already completed or underway before the application, the file is incomplete despite requests for additional documents, or the projected budget is unrealistic. In case of refusal, you receive a reasoned notification.
faq_full.eligibilite_4_q: Can I combine several grants for the same project?
faq_full.eligibilite_4_r: Cumulation is possible within certain limits. The general rule is that total public aid cannot exceed the ceilings set by European de minimis regulations (generally €300,000 over 3 years for SMEs) and cannot exceed 100% of eligible expenditure. Our experts guide you towards the optimal combination.
faq_full.eligibilite_5_q: My project spans several territories. How do I apply?
faq_full.eligibilite_5_r: If your project extends across several territories covered by FEDE, you must submit a separate application for each territorial component. Our experts will guide you to optimise the multi-territorial structuring of your application.
faq_full.frais_1_q: What exactly are the €456 incl. VAT processing fees?
faq_full.frais_1_r: The processing fees (€380 excl. VAT / €456 incl. VAT — 20% VAT) are regulatory administrative fees under Article L1611-2 of the General Code of Local Authorities (CGCT). They cover: administrative analysis and compliance verification, economic and financial expertise by accredited experts, document certification, preparation of the final application to the funding body, and monitoring until submission.
faq_full.frais_2_q: When are the processing fees charged?
faq_full.frais_2_r: The processing fees are only issued once your funding has been formally confirmed by the competent body. You first receive an official funding agreement notification, then an invoice for €456 incl. VAT. Payment of this invoice seals your agreement and triggers the final submission of your application. You never pay before a decision — you pay because you have obtained your grant.
faq_full.frais_3_q: What happens if no funding is granted?
faq_full.frais_3_r: If your application does not result in a funding agreement, no fees will be charged. Our model is simple: the processing fees are the formality confirming an agreement obtained. No agreement, no fees.
faq_full.frais_4_q: How are the processing fees paid?
faq_full.frais_4_r: The processing fees of €456 incl. VAT are payable by bank transfer to FEDE's bank account. The bank details (IBAN, BIC and application reference) are communicated in your official funding agreement notification.
faq_full.frais_5_q: Can I deduct these fees for tax purposes?
faq_full.frais_5_r: Yes, in most cases, processing fees constitute deductible operating expenses for businesses and self-employed workers. An official invoice is provided to justify these expenses to your accountant or tax authorities.
faq_full.delais_1_q: How long does the entire process take, from registration to disbursement?
faq_full.delais_1_r: From creating your account to actual disbursement, the overall timeframe is generally between 45 and 120 days. Key stages: file preparation (1–5 days), administrative review (30–90 days), fee payment and submission (2–5 days), validation by the funding body (15–30 days). A well-prepared file significantly reduces timelines.
faq_full.delais_2_q: How can I track the progress of my application?
faq_full.delais_2_r: Your FEDE personal space centralises all information on your application in real time. Each status change triggers an email notification and a dashboard update. You can also send messages to your advisor directly from your personal space.
faq_full.delais_3_q: What happens if my application is incomplete?
faq_full.delais_3_r: If your application is incomplete, our experts will send you a detailed request specifying missing items. You then have 15 days (extendable on request) to provide the required elements. After this deadline without response, your application may be put on hold or archived.
faq_full.delais_4_q: Is the grant paid in a single payment?
faq_full.delais_4_r: This depends on the funding programme and amount awarded. For small amounts (below €30,000), payment is generally in a single instalment. For larger projects, payment may be staggered in 2 or 3 tranches. The terms are specified in the grant agreement signed with the funding body.
faq_full.documents_1_q: What documents should I prepare before submitting my application?
faq_full.documents_1_r: Essential documents to prepare in advance: a valid identity document (national ID or passport, both sides), proof of address less than 3 months old, bank account details (IBAN) in the name of the promoter, company extract or association statutes, a detailed project description (2 to 5 pages), a financial plan (income/expenditure table), and quotes or cost estimates.
faq_full.documents_2_q: Are my documents secure on the platform?
faq_full.documents_2_r: Document security is an absolute priority. All transmissions are encrypted via TLS 1.3. Documents are stored on secure servers located in France, in compliance with GDPR. Access is strictly limited to experts handling your application and agents of competent funding bodies.
faq_full.documents_3_r: Accepted file formats include: PDF (recommended), JPEG and PNG for document photos, DOCX for text documents, and XLSX for financial spreadsheets. Each file must not exceed 20 MB.
faq_full.documents_4_q: Is my personal data protected in accordance with GDPR?
faq_full.documents_4_r: Yes, FEDE fully complies with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR — EU Regulation 2016/679). You have the right of access, rectification, erasure, portability, and objection. Contact our Data Protection Officer at dpo@fede-financement.com.
faq_full.documents_5_q: Can I modify my application after submitting it?
faq_full.documents_5_r: Once submitted, your application enters the review phase and can no longer be directly modified. Contact your advisor through the messaging system for corrections. Major changes may require submitting a new application.
faq_full.securite_1_q: Does FEDE have an official Telegram channel or Facebook page?
faq_full.securite_1_r: No, absolutely not. FEDE is not present on any mainstream social network and has no official Telegram channel. Any account, Telegram group, Facebook, Instagram, TikTok or WhatsApp page claiming to be FEDE is identity fraud. Report these fraudulent accounts immediately to the authorities and to our team: support@fede-financement.com.
faq_full.securite_2_q: Can FEDE ask me to transfer funds to another account?
faq_full.securite_2_r: Never. FEDE will NEVER ask you to transfer funds to a third-party account, top up a card, pay unblocking fees, a payment deposit or customs tax. The only legitimate FEDE fees are the administrative processing fees (€456 incl. VAT), payable exclusively from your personal space on fede-financement.com, and only after official confirmation of your grant. Any other payment request is a scam.
faq_full.securite_3_q: Can a FEDE agent or advisor contact me for my banking information?
faq_full.securite_3_r: No, never. No member of our team will contact you to ask for: your password, bank card number, CVV, SMS validation code, full bank details or any confidential information. Hang up immediately and report this call to support@fede-financement.com.
faq_full.securite_4_q: How can I identify an official communication from FEDE?
faq_full.securite_4_r: Official FEDE communications come exclusively from email addresses ending in @fede-financement.com. Always check the URL in your browser (https://www.fede-financement.com). We never communicate via third-party messaging apps (WhatsApp, Telegram, Signal) or Gmail/Hotmail/Yahoo addresses. If in doubt, contact us at support@fede-financement.com before acting.
faq_full.securite_5_q: What should I do if I have been a victim of a scam claiming to be FEDE?
faq_full.securite_5_r: Act immediately: 1. Contact your bank to block fraudulent transfers. 2. File a complaint with national police. 3. Report the scam on the relevant government cybercrime reporting site. 4. Inform us at support@fede-financement.com with all details. Keep all evidence: messages, transfer receipts, screenshots.

antiscam_modal.title: Security warning
antiscam_modal.subtitle: Protect yourself from scams — read carefully
antiscam_modal.warning: Malicious individuals are impersonating FEDE to defraud project promoters. This information is essential for your protection.
antiscam_modal.no_telegram: We have no official Telegram channel or Facebook account
antiscam_modal.no_telegram_desc: Any Telegram group, Facebook, Instagram or WhatsApp account claiming to be FEDE is a scam. Report these fraudulent accounts to the authorities immediately.
antiscam_modal.no_wire: We will never ask you to transfer funds
antiscam_modal.no_wire_desc: Any request to transfer funds to a third party, top up an account, pay unblocking fees, a deposit or customs tax is fraud. The only legitimate FEDE fees (€456 incl. VAT) are paid exclusively on this platform, after official confirmation of your grant.
antiscam_modal.no_data: No agent, advisor or partner will contact you for your data
antiscam_modal.no_data_desc: Our FEDE Advisors, partner agents and team members will never ask for: your password, bank card number, SMS code, full bank details or any confidential banking information by phone, SMS or external messaging.
antiscam_modal.official_channels_desc: Always check the URL in your browser. Our official communications come only from @fede-financement.com. If in doubt: support@fede-financement.com
antiscam_modal.understood: I understand — Access the platform safely
antiscam_modal.once: This message is displayed once only · Support: support@fede-financement.com

processus_content.step1_title: Create your account and verify eligibility
processus_content.step1_desc: Everything begins with creating your secure personal space on FEDE. This step is entirely free and without commitment.
processus_content.step1_tip: Your account gives you access to all your applications, documents, messages and real-time notifications. No software installation required.
step1_steps: Enter your personal information (name, first name, email address, telephone)
Select your territory from the eligible territories
Choose your project promoter status (sole trader, company, association, local authority)
Validate your email address via the confirmation link sent automatically
processus_content.step2_title: Build and submit your funding application
processus_content.step2_desc: From your personal space, you build your application by providing all the information required for its review.
processus_content.step2_tip: A guided form supports you at every step. If you have any doubt about a field, our support team responds within 24h.
step2_steps: Describe your project: title, sector, objectives and expected impacts
Specify your territory and the targeted funding programme
Indicate the total cost of your project and your personal contribution
Attach a detailed description of your projected financial plan
Submit your application for pre-administrative validation
processus_content.step3_duree: 1 to 5 days depending on document availability
processus_content.step3_desc: Once your application is created, you must upload all required supporting documents via your secure personal space. All common formats are accepted (PDF, JPG, PNG, DOCX).
processus_content.step3_tip: The exact list of documents varies by programme and territory. Missing documents generate an alert in your space. Each document is encrypted during transfer and stored on secure servers.
step3_steps: Log in to your personal space
Go to the My documents section
Upload each document in the corresponding section
Check that each document is legible and currently valid
Validate the completeness of your document file
step3_docs: Valid identity document (national ID or passport, both sides)
Proof of address less than 3 months old
Bank account details (IBAN) in the name of the promoter
Company extract (companies) or statutes (associations) less than 3 months old
Tax returns for the last 2 financial years (if existing business)
processus_content.step4_desc: This is the central stage of the process. Our accredited experts conduct an in-depth analysis of your application according to the eligibility criteria of the targeted programme.
processus_content.step4_tip: At each key stage of the review, you receive an email notification and an update in your personal space. If the file is incomplete, our experts will contact you to request the necessary additional documents.
step4_steps: Administrative verification: compliance of documents and declared information
Eligibility analysis: match between your project and programme criteria
Economic and financial expertise: viability, relevance and impact of the project
Certification of supporting documents by our accredited experts
Preparation of the final application for the funding body
Issuance of the review notification (positive or negative with reasons)
processus_content.step5_desc: Once your funding is confirmed by the competent body, the regulatory processing fees are issued in accordance with Article L1611-2 of the CGCT. These fees are the official formality that seals the agreement.
processus_content.step5_tip: These fees confirm that you have obtained what you applied for. They are never issued before your funding is formally granted.
step5_steps: Receipt of the official funding confirmation notification
Issuance of an official invoice of €380 excl. VAT / €456 incl. VAT confirming the agreement
Payment by bank transfer to the FEDE account provided in the official notification
Confirmation of transfer received and administrative finalisation of the file
Submission of the certified file to the funding body for disbursement
step5_docs: Official FEDE invoice (€380 excl. VAT / €456 incl. VAT)
processus_content.step6_desc: After payment of processing fees, your finalised file is forwarded to the competent funding body for final validation and payment processing.
processus_content.step6_tip: Payment is made in full or in several instalments according to the programme terms. You are notified by email as soon as the transfer is initiated.
step6_steps: Submission of the certified file to the funding body (ERDF, ESF+, national funds, etc.)
Final validation by the body's selection committee
Issuance of the official grant agreement
Electronic signature of the agreement via your personal space
Grant disbursement to your bank account (IBAN provided)
processus_content.g1_desc: All communications and payments go through TLS/SSL encrypted connections. Your data is hosted in France.
processus_content.g2_desc: Each stage of your application triggers an email notification and an update to your personal space.
processus_content.g3_desc: We are committed to respecting the announced review timelines. Any overrun is reported to you with an explanation.
processus_content.g4_desc: Our support team is available Monday to Friday from 8am to 6pm to answer all your questions.

accuse.title: Acknowledgement of Receipt
accuse.subtitle: Non-repayable funding application
accuse.body1: We acknowledge receipt of your non-repayable funding application submitted on the FEDE platform.
accuse.body2: Your file has been successfully registered in our systems and is currently being reviewed by our teams.
accuse.step1: Our teams are reviewing your file against the criteria of the requested programme.
accuse.step2: A dedicated FEDE advisor will contact you as soon as possible.
accuse.step3: You will receive your eligibility report by email and in your personal account.
accuse.notifications: All your notifications are accessible from your FEDE personal account.

eligibility.organisme_default: To be confirmed based on the selected programme
eligibility.next_steps_text: Our teams are reviewing your file. You will be notified by email of any updates. A FEDE advisor will contact you as soon as possible to share the results of the eligibility analysis.
eligibility.avis_desc: The project meets the eligibility criteria identified at this stage of the analysis.
eligibility.footer_note: Non-repayable public funding · Article L1611-2 CGCT
eligibility.criteria_secteur_desc: Sector eligible for the selected programme

contrat.title: Advisory mission agreement
contrat.intro: This contract defines the conditions under which FEDE assists the Client in obtaining non-repayable public funding.
contrat.duree_note: Renewable by written agreement of the parties.
contrat.paiement_note: Fees due upon receipt of the first grant award notification.
contrat.cgct: In accordance with Article L1611-2 CGCT.
contrat.eng_prestataire_intro: Identify funding programmes for which the project is eligible.
contrat.eng_p1: Prepare and submit files to the relevant funding bodies.
contrat.eng_p2: Monitor applications and keep the Client informed of any developments.
contrat.eng_p3: Forward to the Client any decision made by the funding bodies.
contrat.eng_client_intro: Provide all necessary documents within the required timeframes.
contrat.eng_c1: Certify the accuracy of all information provided.
contrat.eng_c2: Inform the Service provider of any change in circumstances that may affect the project's eligibility.
contrat.eng_c3: Pay fees within the agreed timeframes.
contrat.cadre_confidential: The Parties undertake to treat confidentially all information exchanged under this contract.
contrat.cadre_rgpd: Personal data is processed in accordance with the GDPR (EU Regulation 2016/679) and applicable data protection law.
contrat.cadre_resiliation: Either Party may terminate the contract with 30 days' written notice. In the event of serious breach, termination without notice.
contrat.cadre_force_majeure: Neither Party shall be held liable in the event of a force majeure event (Article 1218 of the French Civil Code).
contrat.cadre_droit: This contract is governed by French law. In the event of a dispute, the competent courts of Paris shall have sole jurisdiction.

fiche.title: Supplementary information form
fiche.instruction: Please complete all sections below and return this signed document to your FEDE advisor as soon as possible.
fiche.f6: Name and title of legal representative
fiche.rgpd_note1: The information collected is necessary for preparing your funding application file.
fiche.rgpd_note2: It is processed in accordance with the GDPR (EU Regulation 2016/679) and applicable data protection law.

notification.title: Award notification
notification.subtitle: Non-repayable funding application
notification.body: We are pleased to inform you that your funding application has been accepted. Below you will find a summary of the decision and important information to comply with.
notification.obligations_intro: By accepting this grant, you undertake to:
notification.obl1: Use the funds exclusively for the purposes of the project described in your application.
notification.obl2: Comply with the completion deadlines agreed with the funding body.
notification.obl3: Provide expenditure receipts and progress reports at the set deadlines.
notification.obl4: Inform the funding body of any significant change affecting the project.
notification.frais_body: Following this favourable decision, FEDE processing fees amount to:
notification.frais_legal: in accordance with the signed mission contract and Article L1611-2 of the General Code of French Local Authorities.
notification.frais_modal: Payment details will be communicated to you separately.
notification.recours_body: In accordance with applicable regulations, any appeal against this decision must be filed within two (2) months of its notification. You may file a friendly appeal with FEDE or a contentious appeal before the competent court.
notification.felicitations: We congratulate you on this important milestone and remain at your disposal to support you in the success of your project.

facture.title: INVOICE
facture.article: Article L1611-2 of the General Code of French Local Authorities
facture.designation: Advisory mission for non-repayable public funding
facture.description: Support for research, identification and preparation of funding files.
facture.paiement_body: Payment by bank transfer or credit card.
facture.paiement_ref: Please quote the file reference when making payment.
facture.mention1: Any dispute must be submitted in writing within 30 days of receipt of this invoice.
facture.mention2: Late payment penalties will apply in accordance with applicable law.
